tools.name: validate_cuit
tools.summary: Validate an Argentine CUIT/CUIL via the AFIP modulo-11 check digit algorithm.

cuit.description: The CUIT/CUIL to validate. Accepts any format with or without separators (20-X-Y, 20.X.Y, 20 X Y, 20XY).

output.personType: fisica_masculina | fisica_femenina | fisica_extranjera | juridica | juridica_alternativa | desconocida
output.error: string | null (Spanish actionable error message; surface verbatim to users)

whenToUse: User pasted a CUIT/CUIL and wants to know if it's valid
Need to detect typos in a user-supplied CUIT
Need to infer person type (físcia vs jurídica) from a CUIT prefix
Need to normalize CUIT formatting before downstream operations
whenNotToUse: User wants the taxpayer's name, tax condition, or monotributo category — use lookup_cuit_afip instead

tools.name: lookup_cuit_afip
tools.summary: Look up an Argentine CUIT against AFIP's padron webservice.

cuit.description: The CUIT/CUIL to look up. Pass the validated/normalized form (output of validate_cuit.normalized).

output.error: string | null (when available is false, contains setup steps; surface verbatim)

whenToUse: User wants the taxpayer's full legal name
User wants to know the tax condition (Monotributo, Responsable Inscripto, etc.)
User wants the monotributo category (A through K)
User wants the registered fiscal address or activities
whenNotToUse: Just for format validation — use validate_cuit (cheaper, no I/O)
When validate_cuit returned valid: false — the AFIP lookup will fail too, save the request

errorPatterns.shape: { available: false, error: 'AFIP padron lookup not configured...' }
errorPatterns.meaning: App hasn't wired an AFIP cert; the adapter is the default UnconfiguredAfipPadronAdapter
errorPatterns.recovery: Surface the error message verbatim to the user — it contains setup steps. Do not invent taxpayer info.
